title: The Activation-Based Learning Recap
concept: We have covered zero/few-shot prompting, activation-based context learning, emergent capacities, and chain-of-thought logic paths.

exercises.prompt: What is it called when you give an AI a few examples of a task to help it understand what you want?
exercises.explain: Few-shot prompting provides a few examples of the task within the prompt to guide the AI's behavior.

cards.title: Instant Learning!
cards.body: Did you know AI models can learn new tricks instantly without updating a single line of their permanent code? Let's recap how activation-based learning and smart prompting make this magic happen!

cards.title: What is Activation-Based Learning?
cards.body: Activation-based learning is when an AI learns to perform a new task on the fly, simply by reading the instructions and examples in your prompt. No permanent brain updates (training) needed!

cards.title: The Chef's Recipe
cards.body: Imagine your AI is a master chef. Instead of sending them back to culinary school (fine-tuning), you just hand them a recipe card with a few examples of plated dishes. They immediately catch on and plate the next dish perfectly. That is activation-based learning!

cards.title: Context in Action
cards.body: By giving an AI three examples of translating French slang before asking it to translate a fourth, you are using few-shot activation-based learning. The AI picks up the pattern instantly just from the context.

cards.title: The Retraining Myth
cards.body: Many believe that for an AI to learn a new task, engineers must retrain it with millions of data points. In reality, large models have emergent capacities that let them learn instantly from a well-structured prompt.

cards.title: Prompting vs. Training
cards.body: Let's compare how we change an AI's behavior. We can either show it examples on the fly, or change its core brain permanently.

right.text: Permanently updates the AI's internal model weights using massive computational training.

left.text: Uses prompts, examples, and logic paths inside the context window. Zero permanent changes.

exercises.prompt: Which prompting technique involves asking the AI to perform a task without giving it any examples at all?
exercises.explain: Zero-shot prompting gives the AI a task with absolutely zero examples, relying entirely on its pre-existing knowledge.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Emergent capacities are unexpected abilities that appear in AI models as they grow larger.
exercises.explain: Emergent capacities are abilities that appear automatically in larger models, even though they weren't explicitly trained for them.

exercises.prompt: What technique helps an AI solve complex problems by showing its step-by-step reasoning?
exercises.sentence: We use chain-of-thought prompting to make the AI output its logic path step-by-step.
exercises.explain: Chain-of-thought prompting asks the AI to write out its intermediate thinking steps, which guides it to a more accurate final answer.

exercises.prompt: Which of these outputs demonstrates an AI using a Chain-of-Thought logic path?

options.text: Prompt B: 'Let us think step-by-step: First, we add the base numbers. Second, we multiply...'

exercises.explain: Prompt B shows a clear step-by-step reasoning path (chain-of-thought), whereas Prompt A just guesses and Prompt C uses code blocks but no reasoning.

exercises.prompt: How do activation-based learning and advanced prompting techniques actually guide an AI's behavior during a conversation?

options.text: They permanently rewrite the AI's core codebase and train it on new servers.
They steer active brain pathways using prompt examples and reasoning steps.
They completely disable all emergent capacities to keep the AI simple.
They force the model to memorize a single answer forever without context.

exercises.explain: Few-shot prompting shows the AI examples, chain-of-thought helps it reason, and activation-based learning lets it adapt dynamically without changing its internal weight values.
